titleStmt.title: A Transcription of John in Achmimic
P. Strasb. Copt. 371, 372, 375-385
The New Testament
The Gospel according to John
john

respStmt.name: the International Greek New Testament Project
Christian Askeland
Elina Perttilä

sponsor.name: The Institute for Textual Scholarship and Electronic Editing

publisher.name: The Institute for Textual Scholarship and Electronic Editing
University of Birmingham

availability.p: Licensed under a Creative Commons Attribution 4.0 International License (CC BY 4.0): available for re-use provided attribution is made to the original creators (Christian Askeland and any named participants) and an indication is given of any changes made.

msDesc.surrogates: The images to which this transcription is linked are © Photo. et coll. BNU Strasbourg

sourceDesc.additional: Funk, Wolf-Peter. “Zur Frage der achmimischen Version der Evangelien.” In Coptology: past, present, and future. Studies in honour of Rodolphe Kasser, edited by Søren Giversen, Martin Krause, and Peter Nagel, 327–340. Louvain: Peeters, 1994. Rösch, Friedrich, ed. Bruchstücke des ersten Clemensbriefes nach dem achmimischen Papyrus der Strassburger Universitäts- und Landesbibliothek, mit biblischen Texten derselben Handschrift. Strasbourg: Schlesier and Schweikhardt, 1910. Sethe, Kurt. “Bemerkungen zu W. Till’s Achmimisch-K. Grammatik.” Zeitschrift für ägyptische Sprache und Altertumskunde 64 (1929): 65–71. Sethe, Kurt. “review of Till, Achmîmisch-koptische Grammatik.” Deutsche Literaturzeitung 37 (1928): 1800–1803.

projectDesc.p: This transcription was made by Christian Askeland and Elina Perttilä for the International Greek New Testament Project in its work towards the Editio Critica Maior of John in 2011.

editorialDecl.p: The initial transcription file was a plain text file later converted to XML. Further information about the transcription procedures followed may be found in the electronic edition of the majuscules at
The XML specifications used were version 1.5 of the IGNTP Guidelines available at

revisionDesc.change: Converted to XML specifications 1.5 by Hugh Houghton
Converted to XML by Catherine Smith and Hugh Houghton

body.note: F 1r-50r contained other fragmentary Achmimic texts (1 Clement, a lost text and James). The original manuscript did not contain the first nine chapters of John, and only offered limited pericopes of the Greek. For the time being, this file only contains the Coptic text
